Hedger Pressure Indicator
← Back to dashboard

Hedger Pressure Indicator

CoT hedger pressure: supply expansion & shortage stress.

As of 2026-02-03

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement (mandatory): Given a Supply Pressure Z-score of 0.275 and a Shortage Pressure Z-score of -0.369, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Regime shift.
  • The signal is currently classified in the Normal regime for both supply expansion and shortage stress, indicating commercial positioning is within typical historical bounds (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Regime classification: Robust Z-scores above 0.5 for Hedger_SupplyPressure_z or Hedger_ShortagePressure_z trigger Moderate regimes, while scores above 1.5 trigger High pressure regimes (1).
  • Per-market flags: Deep-short (supply) is flagged at Hedger_zscore ≤ -1.0; Deep-long (shortage) is flagged at Hedger_zscore ≥ 1.0 (1).
  • Normalisation: Uses Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) to compute robust z-scores for daily shares to minimise sensitivity to structural market shifts (1).
  • Dataset: CoT Hedger Pressure daily aggregation; latest observation: 2026-02-03.

Key Dynamics:

  • Supply expansion pressure reached a "Moderate" inflection point (Z=0.503) on 2026-01-06 but has since stabilised within Normal bounds (1).
  • Shortage and physical stress signals are currently subdued, with the hedger_deep_long_share at 0.202 (1).
  • Stabilisation: Both supply and shortage metrics are converging toward the historical mean, suggesting a lack of commercial urgency (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: Hedger_SupplyPressure_z crossing above the 0.50 threshold.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Continued range-bound commercial activity as positioning remains within normal historical standard deviations (1).
  • Most plausible upside risk: Physical shortage stress triggered by hedgers moving to deep-long positions (Z > 0.5).
  • Most plausible downside risk: Supply expansion pressure triggered by producers increasing short hedges (Z > 0.5).

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Tactical (weeks): Based on the weekly reporting frequency of the underlying Commitment of Traders data.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; provides a fundamental commercial anchor to speculative-led sentiment signals.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the Pricing and Supply/Demand macro dimensions by identifying physical market tightness or oversupply (1).
  • Cycle position: Mid-cycle (Normal regime).
  • Interaction: Typically used to cross-check speculative crowding; divergence between specs and hedgers often warns of reversals.

Data & References:

  • CoT Hedger Pressure Aggregation: latest observation date 2026-02-03 (1).
  • Most influential datapoint: Hedger_SupplyPressure_z (0.275) and Hedger_ShortagePressure_z (-0.369).
  • Additional reliability would be improved by incorporating global inventory levels and forward curve backwardation/contango data.

Supply expansion and shortage stress by hedger positioning.
